title: Indirect objects in past requests
subtitle: Say gave me, sent you, told him.
overview: Me, te, le, nos can work with past-tense verbs.
They stay before the conjugated verb.

items.es: Te escribí ayer, pero no respondiste.
items.en: I wrote to you yesterday, but you did not answer.

items.es: El camarero nos trajo la cuenta.
items.en: The waiter brought us the bill.

items.es: Le dije que no tenía tiempo.
items.en: I told him/her that I did not have time.

items.es: Mi amigo me dio un buen consejo.
items.en: My friend gave me good advice.
